module_map.responsibility: Global settings and configuration management; singleton instance holds all settings for the Django project
Application registry and lifecycle; manages AppConfig instances for all installed apps; handles model discovery and imports
Request/response pipeline orchestration; loads middleware chain, handles exceptions, manages async/sync adaptation
HTTP abstractions for request/response semantics; QueryDict parsing, cookie handling, response types
URL routing and view resolution; URL pattern compilation and matching, reverse URL lookup, namespace handling
View dispatch and generic view classes; HTTP method routing, context processing, template response handling
Database abstraction layer; connection pooling, transaction management, ORM query execution
Request/response processors; cross-cutting concerns like security, caching, compression, internationalization
Template loading, compilation, and rendering; context processors, filters, tags, template engine management
Observer pattern implementation; signal emission and receiver binding for decoupled event handling
Framework-level events; request_started, request_finished, pre_save, post_save hooks for extensibility
Utility functions and decorators; text encoding, functional utilities, module loading, logging adapters
Test infrastructure; test case classes, fixtures, client simulation, database isolation
Form abstraction and validation; field types, widget rendering, form rendering and processing

pipeline_trace.description: WSGI application entry point; calls django.setup() to initialize settings and apps registry, returns WSGIHandler callable
WSGIHandler.__call__() creates WSGIRequest from WSGI environ; parses method, path, GET/POST, cookies, headers
load_middleware() constructs middleware chain from settings.MIDDLEWARE; each middleware wraps previous in reverse order; request flows through process_request hooks
URLResolver.resolve(path) matches request.path against URLPattern list; returns ResolverMatch with view function/class and kwargs
View.dispatch() routes to HTTP method handler (get/post/etc); cbv instantiation and method call; passes request and URL kwargs to handler
Model.objects.filter/get/etc instantiate QuerySet; QuerySet.query built as SQL; connection.cursor() executes via backend-specific adapter
View returns HttpResponse or TemplateResponse; TemplateResponse.render() loads template via Loader, compiles, renders with context
Response flows back through middleware in forward order; process_response hooks modify response (compress, add headers, etc)
BaseHandler emits request_finished signal with sender and request; receivers (if any) invoked synchronously or async-wrapped
HttpResponse serialized to WSGI response: (status_code, headers_list, content_iterator); WSGI server sends to client

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central orchestrator of request pipeline; loads and chains all middleware, coordinates signals, handles async/sync adaptation. Every request flows through here. Imports: conf, urls, db, signals, exception handling, logging

ORM hub for data persistence; accessed from views, forms, middleware, signals, admin. Manages connections, transactions, query compilation. Database abstraction layer that multiple app layers depend on
Request/response primitive hub; HttpRequest flows through all stages, HttpResponse is return type from views/middleware. Low-level abstraction consumed by handlers, views, middleware, test client
URL routing hub; called during request pipeline to map paths to views. Depends on views module to hold view functions. Reverse URL lookup used throughout templates and redirects
Application registry hub; apps_ready signal coordination, model discovery, app loading during startup. All components query it for installed app list and model registry

change_proposal.feature_summary: Add an async-compatible structured logging middleware that captures per-request metadata (view name, ORM query count, response time) and emits structured log entries with JSON serialization.
files_to_modify: django/core/handlers/base.py (modify to store request metadata in request object)
django/core/signals.py (ensure request_finished signal passes request state)
django/db/utils.py (optional: add query counter to connection)
new_types: StructuredLoggingMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin) - main middleware class with async_capable=True, process_request/process_response hooks
RequestMetadata(TypedDict) - type hint for per-request state: {view_name, query_count, start_time, response_time, status_code}
StructuredLogEntry(TypedDict) - log event schema: {timestamp, request_id, view_name, query_count, response_ms, status_code, user_id}
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow django.middleware.common.CommonMiddleware and django.middleware.cache.CacheMiddleware patterns: (1) inherit from MiddlewareMixin, (2) implement process_request to capture start_time and initial metadata, (3) implement process_response to compute duration and query count, (4) use django.utils.log.log_response for structured formatting, (5) support async via async_capable=True flag and async def __call__() for async-capable servers
change_proposal.integration_point: Middleware chain integration via settings.MIDDLEWARE list. To capture view name: store ResolverMatch in request object during URL resolution (already done in core.handlers.base), or attach to request during view dispatch. To count ORM queries: hook django.db.connection.queries list (available in DEBUG mode) or use django.test.utils.CaptureQueriesContext approach. Emit structured logs via django.utils.log.getLogger('django.structured') with JSON-formatted LogRecord.extra dict. Hook into request_finished signal optionally for cleanup
risks: Performance risk: query counting requires introspection of django.db.connection.queries which has overhead; mitigation: only enable in specific environments, cache query count per-request rather than counting on-demand
Compatibility risk: ResolverMatch may be None on 404; mitigation: check for None before accessing view_name attribute

Async risk: django.db.connection.queries is thread-local; in async contexts must use thread-safety primitives (asgiref.local.Local); mitigation: follow asgiref patterns used in django.urls.base._urlconfs
Maintainability risk: structured logging format must stay in sync with log consumer schemas; mitigation: define schema in settings and validate on startup
Feature creep risk: temptation to add more metadata (headers, request body, response body) bloats middleware; mitigation: keep minimal set, document extensibility points

tool_usage.params: find command to discover top-level modules and count files
pattern '^from django\.|^import django\.' to count cross-module imports
read django/core/wsgi.py, django/core/handlers/wsgi.py, django/core/handlers/base.py to trace WSGI entry point and middleware chain
read django/urls/base.py to understand URL resolution and ResolverMatch
read django/views/generic/base.py to understand View dispatch pattern
read django/db/models/__init__.py and django/http/__init__.py to identify key types
read django/middleware/common.py to understand MiddlewareMixin pattern
read django/apps/config.py and django/dispatch/dispatcher.py to understand apps registry and signal dispatch
